name: Norris Turney
abstract: Norris William Turney (September 8, 1921 – January 17, 2001) was an American jazz saxophonist and flautist.

interviews.title: Norris Turney interviewed by Monk Rowe, Aspen, Colorado, October 11, 1997

quotes.text: Well we made a recording for the seventieth birthday. I don’t remember whether it was at Carnegie Hall. It probably was.
quotes.caption: Uncertain memory of a 70th birthday recording at Carnegie Hall
quotes.doc_title: Norris Turney interviewed by Monk Rowe, Aspen, Colorado, October 11, 1997

about_quotes.speaker: Frank Foster
about_quotes.evidence: He played with my band in New York. ... I had saxophonist Norris Turney -- was my lead alto player.
about_quotes.doc_title: Frank Foster — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Erskine Hawkins
about_quotes.evidence: Norris Turney. I Mercer Ellington now, think he's playing with (unintelligible) first alto. He took Bobby Smith's place.

about_quotes.speaker: Frank Foster
about_quotes.evidence: And there was a friend of mine who was also an alto saxophonist. And he and I together were studying a score. It was written by a professional who was in his 20s at that time a guy named Norris Turney
about_quotes.doc_title: Frank Foster interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, April 7, 1998

about_quotes.speaker: Howard McGhee
about_quotes.evidence: Norris Turney, Clifford Jordan, Russ Andrews... in the saxophone section. Anyway, mmm, who were the trumpet players...Eddie Preston, Nat Howard...
